meta.description: Europe's specialized boutique law firm for digital rights. Platform suspensions, DSA complaints, GDPR damages, consumer protection for digital content. Free first assessment within 24h.

hero.headline: Your digital rights. Enforced.
hero.sub: Suspended, frozen, removed? LexRights is Europe's specialized boutique for digital rights — EU bar admissions plus New York Bar counsel for cross-border matters. Free first assessment within 24 hours.

pillars.title: Four pillars under one roof
pillars.subtitle: We combine platform specialization with European legal expertise. Each pillar with deep, specific content - never generic legal advice.

platforms.title: Platform suspensions
platforms.desc: Account bans on gaming, social, marketplace, payment and crypto platforms. We enforce your reinstatement and damages claims.

dsa.title: DSA & Digital Services
dsa.desc: Complaints under Art. 17, 20, 21 DSA. Statement-of-Reasons analysis, internal complaint handling, referral to certified Out-of-Court Dispute Bodies.

gdpr.title: GDPR & Privacy
gdpr.desc: Data access requests, non-material damages following ECJ C-300/21, right to be forgotten, prohibition of solely automated decisions under Art. 22 GDPR.

consumer.title: Digital consumer rights
consumer.desc: Consumer protection for digital content and services: in-game purchases, streaming, app stores, cloud - under § 327 et seq. BGB / Directive 2019/770.

platforms.title: Specialized for every platform
platforms.subtitle: Dedicated deep-content pages per platform - not one-size-fits-all. We know the ToS, anti-cheat systems, complaint paths and case law.

steps.title: Free first assessment
steps.desc: You describe your case. We assess the chances within 24 hours - at no cost, no commitment.

steps.title: Strategy & fee agreement
steps.desc: On engagement you receive a clear strategic plan and transparent fee agreement - fixed fee where possible.

steps.title: Out-of-court enforcement
steps.desc: GDPR data request, DSA complaint, formal letter to the platform with deadline. Often successful at this stage.

steps.title: Escalation: ADR or court
steps.desc: If needed: referral to Out-of-Court Dispute Body (DSA Art. 21) or interim injunction at consumer's home venue.

items.title: Specialized, not generalist
items.desc: We don't do generic IT law. Every platform, every legal topic with dedicated deep content and experience.

items.title: DSA-native
items.desc: First firm with an explicit workflow for the Digital Services Act. Statement-of-Reasons analysis, ADR referral, damages under Art. 54 DSA.

items.title: EU-wide single source
items.desc: Consumer's home venue under Brussels Ia. We represent you across DACH and the EU in German and English.

items.title: Digital-first
items.desc: Video call, beA, online file. No office visits required. All mandates handled digitally.

items.title: Transparent
items.desc: Clear strategy, clear fee agreement before engagement. Direct billing with legal expenses insurance possible.

items.title: Specialist team
items.desc: PhD platform-law expert with Oxford background + privacy specialist with Tilburg LL.M. - complementary depth.

cta.title: Your account suspended? You have digital rights.
cta.subtitle: We assess your case in 24 hours - free and without commitment.

meta.title: Firm
meta.description: LexRights is a pan-European boutique law firm for digital rights. Specialized in platform suspensions, DSA, GDPR and consumer protection for digital content.

hero.title: A law firm for a new legal reality.
hero.sub: LexRights was founded with a clear mission: give consumers and businesses across Europe access to specialized legal protection in the digital space - at eye level with the major platforms.

items.title: Specialization
items.desc: We don't do generic IT law. Our mandates focus consistently on digital rights: platforms, DSA, GDPR, digital content.

items.title: Depth over breadth
items.desc: Dedicated deep analysis per platform and legal topic. We know the ToS, the anti-cheat systems, the complaint paths and the relevant case law.

items.title: EU-wide single source
items.desc: Consumer's home venue under Brussels Ia. We represent you across DACH and the EU in German and English - no correspondent lawyer surcharge.

items.title: Digital-first
items.desc: Video call, beA, secure online file. Mandates handled fully digitally. No office visits required.

items.title: Transparent
items.desc: Clear fee agreement before engagement. Direct billing with legal-expenses insurance. No hidden costs.

team.title: Our team
team.subtitle: Complementary specialization. Double depth in platform law and data protection.

imprint.title: Legal Notice
imprint.regulationsBody: These regulations are available at www.brak.de.

imprint.vsbgBody: We are neither willing nor obliged to participate in dispute resolution proceedings before a consumer arbitration board (§ 36 VSBG). For consumer disputes arising from the mandate relationship, the Schlichtungsstelle der Rechtsanwaltschaft (Neue Grünstraße 17, 10179 Berlin, www.schlichtungsstelle-der-rechtsanwaltschaft.de) is competent.

imprint.liabilityBody: The contents of this website have been created with the greatest care. However, we cannot guarantee the accuracy, completeness or topicality of the content. As a service provider, we are responsible for our own content on these pages in accordance with general laws (§ 7 (1) TMG).

meta.title: Cross-Border US ⇄ EU — Digital Disputes | LexRights
meta.description: EU-qualified counsel with New York Bar partner. We handle digital disputes where a US platform meets an EU user — Brussels Ia jurisdiction, DSA enforcement, US-side coordination.

hero.title: When a US platform meets an EU user, jurisdiction matters.
hero.sub: LexRights combines EU bar admissions with New York Bar counsel. We litigate digital disputes on both sides of the Atlantic — and refuse the matters that don't belong on our desk.

intro.p1: Most platform disputes today are cross-border by design. The user lives in Munich, the platform is incorporated in Delaware, the data sits on AWS Frankfurt, and the terms of service pick California law and a Bay Area arbitrator. The legal question is rarely "what does the law say" — it is "which law, in which forum, enforced how."
intro.p2: Our focus is narrow and deliberate: the EU side of US-facing platform disputes, and the US side of EU-originated matters. We do not practice US domestic law, and we do not pretend to. For matters that fall outside our lane, we say so — and refer.

useCases.title: Where we add value
useCases.subtitle: Three patterns we see repeatedly — and where our combined bar admissions make a measurable difference.

items.title: EU users against US platforms
items.desc: Account terminated, content removed, payouts frozen. The TOS says "California, JAMS arbitration" — but Brussels Ia Art. 17/18 and the DSA say otherwise.
bullets: Forum at the consumer's home court despite US choice-of-law clauses
DSA Art. 14/16/17/20/21 enforcement against VLOPs and intermediaries
GDPR damages claims after CJEU C-300/21 (Österreichische Post)
Coordinated pressure on the US parent through our NY counsel

items.title: US companies serving the EU
items.desc: If you have EU users, the EU has rules for you — and they are enforced. We close the gap between US legal instincts and EU compliance reality.
bullets: DSA notice-and-action workflows, transparency reports, trusted-flagger handling
GDPR Article 27 representative, DPIA review, cross-border SCC mapping
P2B Regulation compliance for marketplaces and ranking algorithms
Member-state consumer-law translation (UWG, BGB §§ 327 ff., NIS2 readiness)

items.title: US-side coordination for EU clients
items.desc: When an EU mandate develops a US flank — discovery, asset tracing, parallel litigation — we keep one matter, one team, one bill.
bullets: Letters Rogatory and 28 U.S.C. § 1782 discovery for EU proceedings
Coordination with US co-counsel for state court and federal litigation
Hague Service & enforcement of EU judgments against US-based assets
Pre-suit demand strategy under DMCA § 512 and Section 230 frameworks

counsel.bio: Blaine joined LexRights as Senior Counsel (US) after a decade between Stanford Law, a Ninth Circuit clerkship, and Wilson Sonsini's tech litigation group in Palo Alto. He is admitted in New York and focuses exclusively on the US dimension of our cross-border practice — Section 230, DMCA, CCPA/CPRA, BIPA, and FTC / state-AG enforcement. He does not handle US matters disconnected from our EU practice.

scope.title: What we do — and what we don't
scope.subtitle: Clarity protects you. We are German and US lawyers; we are not a one-stop US firm.
inScope: EU litigation against US platforms (Brussels Ia, DSA, GDPR)
DSA / GDPR / P2B compliance for US-based companies serving the EU
US-side proceedings tied to an EU mandate (NY State court, federal where applicable)
Pre-suit demand letters under US statutory frameworks (DMCA, Section 230, CCPA)
Cross-border discovery (§ 1782), Hague Service, judgment enforcement
outOfScope: Pure US-domestic disputes with no EU element
US immigration, criminal defense, family or estate matters
US tax, securities, or M&A work
Litigation in states or federal circuits where we lack admission
scope.outOfScopeNote: For matters outside our scope we refer to vetted US co-counsel. We will not take a retainer for work we cannot deliver at the standard our EU mandates expect.

items.q: Can you actually represent me in a US court?
items.a: In New York state court, yes — through Blaine Phillips, admitted to the New York State Bar. For other states or for federal litigation outside the Southern and Eastern Districts of New York, we co-counsel with a vetted US firm and remain lead on the EU side.

items.q: I am a US-based creator with my channel blocked in the EU. Is that something for you?
items.a: Yes — that is a textbook case for us. Your dispute touches DSA Art. 17 (statement of reasons) and Art. 20 (internal complaint handling), and likely Brussels Ia jurisdiction questions if your audience is EU-based. We handle the EU enforcement; Blaine handles US-side correspondence with the platform's US counsel.

items.q: We are a Series B US company about to launch in Germany. Where do we start?
items.a: Pre-launch DSA and GDPR readiness review, Article 27 representative appointment if you have no EU establishment, and a translated terms-of-service that survives § 305c BGB scrutiny. Expect 4–6 weeks of focused work, not a 6-month engagement.

items.q: Do you handle pure US-domestic matters?
items.a: No. If your matter has no EU element, we refer to US co-counsel — usually a boutique we have worked with before. It is the professionally responsible answer, and it is the answer that keeps our cross-border practice sharp.

items.q: How does engagement work practically — time zones, billing, language?
items.a: Initial consultation by video in CET or ET, in English or German. Engagement letters in English with a German-law side letter where required. Billing in EUR; USD on request via our US correspondent account. All matters handled digitally — no office visits required.

items.q: What about the UK, Switzerland, or other non-EU jurisdictions?
items.a: Switzerland, Liechtenstein, and the EEA we handle directly or through our DACH network. UK matters post-Brexit are handled in coordination with London solicitors where the matter is UK-litigated; we lead where the matter is EU-anchored.

cta.title: One mandate. Two bars. One bill.
cta.subtitle: Tell us the matter. We will tell you, within 24 hours, whether it belongs on our desk — and if not, who should have it.
